channel.title: Nature on Здесь будет название
channel.description: Recent content in Nature on Здесь будет название

item.title: The Call of the Wild
item.description:
Buck did not read the newspapers, or he would have known that trouble was brewing. It was not the cold that he feared, but the unknown.

“The wild still lingered in him and the wolf in him merely slept.”

Things he learned:

  • How to survive
  • How to fight
  • How to lead

Read more about nature adventures.

item.title: The Night Born
item.description:
She was night-born, whelped in darkness during a winter storm that howled around the den like a living thing. Her mother named her with a sound that humans could not reproduce — a name that meant something like “shadow-who-runs-silent” in the language of wolves.

From her first moments, she was different. While her littermates tumbled and played in the growing light of spring, she sat apart, watching, learning. Her eyes held depths that the other pups lacked, an ancient wisdom that seemed impossible in one so young.

item.title: White Fang
item.description:
The she-wolf ran alongside the sled. In the morning, men found the dog gone.

The stages of survival:

  1. First, find shelter
  2. Then, find food
  3. Finally, find your pack

A classic tale of the wilderness.

item.title: Brown Wolf
item.description: Brown Wolf came to them in the night, a shadow slipping into their California camp, half-starved and wary as any wild thing. Walt and Madge Irvine nursed him back to health, and in return, he gave them a loyalty that seemed absolute — until the day the call of the North reclaimed him.

item.title: The Northern Lights
item.description: The northern lights danced across the sky in ribbons of green and purple, a celestial display that never failed to inspire awe. The miners stopped their work to watch. The dogs lifted their heads from their paws. Even the most hardened sourdoughs felt something stir in their chests at the sight.

item.title: The White Silence
item.description:
The white silence of the North was unlike any quiet known to civilization. It was not merely an absence of sound but a presence in itself — vast, ancient, indifferent. Men who traveled through it felt the weight of it pressing down upon them, demanding respect, punishing hubris.

Carmen and the kid traveled through this silence, their sled dogs panting in the thin, cold air. The temperature had dropped steadily for three days, and now it hovered somewhere around sixty below. At these temperatures, the very air seemed to crystallize, and breath froze in beards and on eyelashes.

item.title: The Sounding of the Call
item.description: The sounding of the call came more frequently now, and Buck found it increasingly difficult to resist. It came in the howling of wolves on distant ridges. It came in the rustle of wind through ancient spruces. It came in dreams that left him restless and yearning for something he could not name.
